title: Override "equals(Object obj)" and "hashCode()" in Pairs
description: In Java, the equals(Object) and hashCode() methods have a contractual relationship. If two objects are equal according to equals(Object), then calling hashCode() on both should yield the same integer. To follow this contract, both methods should either be inherited or overridden. Improving distinction between unequal objects' hashCodes can enhance hashtable performance.
recommendation: Ensure that if equals(Object) is overridden, hashCode() is also overridden, and vice versa. Improve distinction between unequal objects' hashCodes for better hashtable performance.
impact: Ignoring this rule can lead to unexpected behavior in collections, resulting in potential data loss and bugs in the program.

checks.message: equals() and hashCode() should be overridden together
